title: Of Christ’s Coming to Judgment.

source.title: NPNF1-03. On the Holy Trinity; Doctrinal Treatises; Moral Treatises

verses.book: A Treatise on Faith and the Creed.
verses.text: We believe also, that at the most seasonable time He Will Come from Thence, and Will Judge the Quick and the Dead: whether by these terms are signified the righteous and sinners, or whether it be the case that those persons are here called the quick, whom at that period He shall find, previous to [their] death, upon the earth, while the dead denote those who shall rise again at His advent.

verses.book: A Treatise on Faith and the Creed.
verses.text: This temporal dispensation not only is, as holds good of that generation which respects His being God, but also hath been and shall be.

verses.book: A Treatise on Faith and the Creed.
verses.text: For our Lord hath been upon the earth, and at present He is in heaven, and [hereafter] He shall be in His brightness as the Judge of the quick and the dead.

verses.book: A Treatise on Faith and the Creed.
verses.text: For He shall yet come, even so as He has ascended, according to the authority which is contained in the Acts of the Apostles.

verses.book: A Treatise on Faith and the Creed.
verses.text: It is in accordance with this temporal dispensation, therefore, that He speaks in the Apocalypse, where it is written in this wise: “These things saith He, who is, and who was, and who is to come.”
